name: Retell AI Conversational Voice API
what_it_does: Provides a real-time WebSocket-based voice AI platform for building conversational phone agents with sub-500ms latency, configurable LLM and voice, and function calling during live conversations.
use_cases: Ultra-low-latency AI customer service agents where conversation naturalness is critical
Outbound sales and cold-calling agents that need to handle objections in real time
AI medical intake agents collecting patient symptoms and history before a doctor visit
Restaurant reservation and order-taking agents embedded in phone systems
Real-time coaching or role-play training agents delivered over phone calls
not_for: Asynchronous batch call processing or bulk audio transcription jobs
Non-voice chatbot workflows (web chat, SMS, email automation)
Applications requiring on-premises or private-cloud voice AI with no third-party dependency
best_when: Conversational naturalness and low turn-taking latency are the top priority and you need a managed platform that handles telephony infrastructure, ASR, LLM orchestration, and TTS in a single integrated loop.
avoid_when: You need fine-grained control over each pipeline component (swap in custom ASR models, custom voice clones, or self-hosted LLMs) or require a fully REST-based integration without WebSocket handling.

auth.notes: Single API key used for REST calls and WebSocket authentication. Key is passed as a Bearer token. No scoping — full account access on a single key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial credits on signup (approximately $10 equivalent)
pricing.paid_tiers: ~$0.07–$0.15/minute depending on LLM and voice provider selected; volume discounts available on enterprise plans
pricing.notes: Pricing bundles STT, LLM, and TTS into per-minute rate by default. Bring-your-own LLM key option available to reduce costs. Telephony (phone number) fees apply separately.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent call limits apply; default tier supports ~10–20 concurrent calls; higher limits available on enterprise

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors return JSON with code and message fields. WebSocket disconnects require reconnect logic; error codes in socket events are not always well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys on agent or call creation endpoints. Duplicate call creation creates separate billable calls; agents must guard against retry-induced duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: WebSocket connection must be established within a short window after call creation or the call drops; agents orchestrating async setup steps may miss this window
Function call results must be sent back over the WebSocket in a specific JSON event format; returning results via a separate REST call does not work
Voice interruption (barge-in) handling is automatic but can be overly aggressive; agents may need to tune sensitivity to avoid the AI cutting off mid-sentence on background noise
Call transcripts available post-call may differ from real-time interim transcripts used for function argument extraction, leading to subtle discrepancies in agent logs

Agent configuration (LLM prompt, voice, tools) is set at agent-creation time and requires creating a new agent or updating the agent object before the next call; runtime prompt injection mid-call is limited

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope granularity is a risk in multi-tenant or multi-team setups. HIPAA BAA available on enterprise plan. SOC 2 Type II reported. WebSocket connections use WSS (TLS enforced).
